description_long: This is a freestanding kitchen oven with a white and black color scheme, positioned on a wooden floor with a small patterned rug nearby. The oven features a front-facing glass door with a white handle, and a control panel above the door equipped with five round knobs. The cooktop has four gas burners with black grates, and the oven's sides are plain white, while the rear is metallic with visible ventilation slots and a power cord. The oven appears to be made primarily of metal, with glass in the door and plastic or metal knobs. Its compact size and standard design suggest it is suitable for residential or small commercial use.
description: A compact, freestanding oven with a white body, black glass door, and four-burner cooktop, featuring a front control panel with five knobs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass oven door, white handle, and five control knobs on the top panel.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain white side panel and partial view of the cooktop.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a metallic back with ventilation slots and a visible power cord.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the white side panel and part of the cooktop.
attribute_control_panel_design: The control panel is located above the oven door and features five evenly spaced round knobs, likely for controlling the burners and oven settings. The panel is white, matching the oven body, with no visible digital displays or touchscreens. The knobs are simple and functional, with no apparent labeling or decorative elements visible in the images.
attribute_door_handle_type: The oven door handle is a horizontally mounted, protruding bar style, finished in white to match the oven body. It appears to be made of metal or coated plastic, with a simple, ergonomic design and no decorative features.
attribute_window_presence: present; a large rectangular glass window is centered in the oven door, occupying most of the door's surface.
attribute_ventilation_features: The rear panel has a set of rectangular ventilation slots near the bottom, likely for heat dissipation.
